name: Daily Challenge
description: A profile-level challenge that rotates on a scheduled cadence, often using fixed modifiers, seeds, objectives, rewards, or leaderboard eligibility.
design_notes: Daily challenges need a trusted clock and deterministic setup. Designers should define challenge identity, rotation seed, start and end timestamps, missed-claim policy, offline behavior, reward claim state, leaderboard eligibility, and whether players get one attempt or many.
design_purpose: Give players a fresh recurring goal without adding new content every day.
Create shared community comparison through fixed seeds, modifiers, or score rules.
Encourage return visits while preserving fair reward claim and anti-cheat boundaries.
player_fantasy: The player feels they are taking on today's shared test and comparing their result against friends or the community.

parameters.name: daily_challenge_id
parameters.description: Stable identifier for the current daily challenge.

parameters.name: challenge_rotation_seed
parameters.description: Seed used to choose modifiers, layouts, rewards, or objectives.

parameters.name: challenge_start_timestamp_ms
parameters.description: Server-clock opening timestamp for eligibility and display.

parameters.name: challenge_end_timestamp_ms
parameters.description: Server-clock closing timestamp for attempts and reward claims.

parameters.name: claim_transaction_id
parameters.description: Idempotent operation that prevents duplicate reward grants.

parameters.name: reward_claim_state
parameters.description: Profile state for completion rewards and missed-claim handling.

parameters.name: leaderboard_id
parameters.description: Optional ranking target for challenge score submissions.

edge_cases: A player starts online, finishes offline, and reconnects after challenge_end_timestamp_ms.
Device clock differs from the server clock and shows the wrong challenge window.
The reward is claimable but the content definition changes before the claim transaction completes.
A leaderboard submission arrives after the daily board has closed.
Cross-save profiles complete the same daily challenge on two devices before sync.
common_bugs: Daily challenge content is generated from local date and differs by time zone.
Rewards can be claimed multiple times because claim_transaction_id is not persisted before grant.
Leaderboard entries accept scores from modified clients without anti_cheat_validation.
Challenge UI shows the next daily while the backend still accepts the previous one.
Offline completion overwrites a better online result after late synchronization.
balancing_notes: Daily rewards should motivate return visits without feeling mandatory for core progression.
One-attempt dailies make leaderboards cleaner but can punish disconnects and accessibility interruptions.
Rotating modifiers should vary play style without requiring obscure build knowledge every day.
accessibility_notes: Show start and end timestamps in local-friendly text and avoid relying on countdown color alone.
Offer readable objective summaries and allow players to review modifiers before starting.
Consider practice mode or unranked retries for players who need to learn the challenge setup.

implementation_notes.unity: Fetch daily_challenge_id, challenge_rotation_seed, and server timestamps from a backend or deterministic service before creating content. Persist reward_claim_state and claim_transaction_id locally and remotely before granting currency or items.
implementation_notes.godot: Store challenge definitions as Resources but resolve the active challenge from server-clock data or signed schedule files. Save local attempt state with profile_id so offline attempts can sync without duplicating claims.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use Online Subsystem or backend services for leaderboard_id, score submission, and platform account checks. Server authority should validate challenge ids, submission windows, and anti-cheat tokens before accepting ranked results.
implementation_notes.web: Avoid deriving eligibility from browser clock alone. Use server-issued challenge payloads, store attempt and claim states in account storage, and treat late syncs as reconciliation events rather than fresh completions.

example_games.title: Spelunky 2
example_games.note: Uses daily challenge runs for shared seeded competition.

example_games.title: Slay the Spire
example_games.note: Uses daily climb runs with modifiers and leaderboards.
